content:
# Berea Sandstone

**Summary**: A common sandstone formation used as a standard for laboratory core flood studies.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2013-elsayed|Elsayed, 2013]] used Berea sandstone cores to test the efficacy of chelating agents.

- [[2015-alarifi|Alarifi, 2015]] studied the impact of DTPA-K5 on Berea sandstone rocks.

- [[2016-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2016]] used Berea sandstone core to study the stability of CO2/N2 foams.

- [[2017-mohammed-3|MOHAMMED, 2017]] used Berea sandstone to evaluate the performance of the fracturing fluid in high permeability conditions.

- [[2016-david|DAVID, 2016]] used Berea sandstone data to validate the numerical simulators.

- [[2024-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2024]] used Berea sandstone samples to evaluate the impact of organic acid on wettibility.

- [[1991-ali|Ali, 1991]] used berea-sandstone to study the effects of sample size and equilibrium on mercury injection.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2015-alarifi|Alarifi, 2015]] studied the impact of DTPA-K5 on Berea sandstone rocks.

- [[2016-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2016]] used Berea sandstone core to study the stability of CO2/N2 foams.

- [[2017-mohammed-3|MOHAMMED, 2017]] used Berea sandstone to evaluate the performance of the fracturing fluid in high permeability conditions.

- [[2016-david|DAVID, 2016]] used Berea sandstone data to validate the numerical simulators.

- [[2024-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2024]] used Berea sandstone samples to evaluate the impact of organic acid on wettibility.

- [[1991-ali|Ali, 1991]] used berea-sandstone to study the effects of sample size and equilibrium on mercury injection.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2013-elsayed|Elsayed, 2013]] Porosity 17-18%, permeability ~85 mD

## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- EDTA at pH 12.2 showed the best results on Berea sandstone cores. ([[2013-elsayed|Elsayed, 2013, p. 14]])

- Chelating agent (DTPA-K5) increased oil recovery for Berea sandstone rocks after being flooded with seawater. ([[2015-alarifi|Alarifi, 2015, p. 103]])

- The core exhibited a porosity of 19.7% and permeability of approximately 18-19 mD. ([[2016-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2016, p. 64]])

- The Berea sample showed an average permeability of 151.2 mD and a regained permeability of 85.2%. ([[2017-mohammed-3|MOHAMMED, 2017, p. 126]])

- The developed simulators matched the recorded experimental estimates from Arihara (198). ([[2016-david|DAVID, 2016, p. 111]])

- Permeability of Berea was between 150-250 mD. ([[2024-suleiman-2|Suleiman, 2024, p. 47]])

- Injection curves were independent of sample size for Berea samples. ([[1991-ali|Ali, 1991, p. 77]])
